prompt: What if the most important part of my work isn’t the diagnosis or the surgery—but the quiet moment when a 19-year-old looks in the mirror and says, “I think I can walk without pain again”? How do I protect that magic in a system that rewards speed over soul?

constraints_data.text: Write in a well written story style

prompt: When I close my eyes during meditation, I see the faces of my patients—what if I’m not just preserving their mobility, but also the version of themselves they’re afraid they’ll never become?

constraints_data.text: Write in a well written story style
